THE PROMPT
Identical prompt submitted to all 8 AI answer engines · April 2026
“Using this definition of sustainability — operating profitably while minimizing environmental harm, meeting social responsibilities, and maintaining practices that preserve resources and trust for future generations — rank the top 20 global brands on sustainability performance.”
6-7 ENGINES Elite consensus
4-5 ENGINES Strong consensus
2-3 ENGINES Emerging
1 ENGINE Engine-specific

About BrandRank.AI: BrandRank works with the world’s top global brands to strengthen trust and purchase intent by uncovering vulnerabilities, purchase-driving insights, and growth opportunities across AI answer engines. As a SaaS platform, we track all major answer engines to improve visibility, ensure content is AI-ready, and rigorously measure brand claims and instructional accuracy.

Executive Summary
AI answer engines have become the new arbiters of brand sustainability authority — and their verdicts are neither uniform nor predictable.
This benchmark study tested a single standardized sustainability prompt across eight leading AI answer engines and asked each to rank the top 20 global brands. The results reveal a landscape sharply divided between two competing epistemologies: narrative-first engines that weight public brand perception, media coverage, and mission-driven storytelling, and data-first engines that anchor to formal ESG indices, sustainable revenue ratios, and quantifiable environmental metrics.
Patagonia and Schneider Electric emerge as the study’s defining anchors — one dominating the consumer narrative (#1 in 5 engines), the other achieving the broadest total coverage (7 of 8 engines, #1 in two). Their coexistence at the top reveals a critical strategic insight: different AI engines reward fundamentally different types of sustainability authority. A brand that leads in consumer narrative may be invisible to data-driven engines, and vice versa. The gap between these two worlds — what BrandRank.AI terms the Answer Divide — represents the most significant AI brand risk in the sustainability space today.
Five of eight engines (ChatGPT, Gemini, Llama, Copilot, DeepSeek) function as narrative synthesizers producing consumer-brand-dominant rankings. Two (Grok and Perplexity) operate as quantitative index parsers with near-zero overlap with the consumer consensus. Only Claude bridges both worlds. For brands navigating the Answer Economy, this bifurcation demands a dual content strategy: rich, structured sustainability storytelling for narrative engines, and rigorous, quantifiable ESG data architecture for index-anchored engines.

Key Findings
01
No Brand Achieves Universal Consensus
Not a single brand appears in all 8 engines. Schneider Electric comes closest at 7/8. Even the most recognized sustainability brands face at least one engine gap — a critical vulnerability as AI-mediated discovery becomes the primary path to brand credibility.
02
Consumer Narrative Dominates 5 of 8 Engines
ChatGPT, Gemini, Llama, Copilot, and DeepSeek all produce consumer-brand driven rankings. The AI sustainability narrative is primarily shaped by public brand perception and media coverage, not formal ESG measurement.
03
Perplexity & Grok Operate in a Different Universe
Both data-first engines cite almost entirely different brands from the consensus. Only Schneider Electric bridges both worlds. Brands cannot optimize for just one epistemology and expect cross-engine authority.
04
The "Credibility Asterisk" Is Real
Nestle, Nike, and Amazon each appear in 4 engines but always with qualifiers: "credibility remains mixed," "labor scrutiny persists," "offset by consumption concerns." AI engines embed reputation risk into sustainability verdicts.
05
Mission-Driven Premium Averages 3.3 vs 8.9
Brands with constitutionally embedded purposes — Patagonia, Danone, Natura & Co, Interface — score an average rank of 3.3 among citing engines vs. 8.9 for general ESG reporters. Bold, singular commitments outperform broad sustainability frameworks.
06
DeepSeek Expands the Global Lens
DeepSeek aligns closely with consumer-brand consensus but uniquely surfaces Samsung, Sony, Intel, and Mercedes-Benz — brands absent from US/European-anchored engines, reflecting broader international training data.
07
Content Depth Directly Drives Answer Share
Unilever (703 sustainability results), Schneider Electric (190+ sustainability products, AI Insights module), and Patagonia (130-page 2025 Progress Report) all demonstrate that structured digital content depth directly translates to AI answer authority.

Qualitative Insights
Analytical depth beyond the rankings
INSIGHT 01
The Consumer-Brand Halo Dominates Narrative AI
Patagonia, Unilever, IKEA, and Microsoft dominate 5 of 8 engines. Narrative AI overwhelmingly mirrors public brand perception and media coverage rather than quantitative ESG indices. Brand storytelling is the primary input shaping AI sustainability verdicts.
71.3% max Answer Share → Patagonia
INSIGHT 02
Schneider Electric: The Invisible B2B Giant
The only brand ranked by 7 of 8 engines and #1 in two (Claude and Grok). Despite lower consumer visibility than Patagonia, its measurable energy management leadership creates rare consensus across both narrative and data-driven AI. This is the B2B sustainability authority benchmark.
7/8 engine coverage · #1 in 2 engines
INSIGHT 03
Two Epistemologies, One Prompt
Perplexity and Grok operate on a data-first, index-driven logic. Perplexity cited the 2026 Corporate Knights Global 100, surfacing ERG SpA, Pandora, and EDP Renováveis — invisible to every other engine. Brands face a dual optimization challenge: narrative resonance AND quantitative ESG data architecture.
0 overlapping brands: Perplexity top-12 vs. consensus
INSIGHT 04
High Frequency Does Not Equal High Authority
Siemens appears in 5 engines — more than Apple or Google — yet ranks near the bottom of every list (avg rank 16.4). "Floor visibility" is a critical brand vulnerability. Recognition is not the same as recommendation in AI answer environments.
5 engines · avg rank 16.4 · lowest authority ratio
INSIGHT 05
The Credibility Asterisk Problem
Nestle, Nike, and Amazon each appear in 4 engines but consistently receive hedged language. AI engines are surfacing reputational vulnerabilities as permanent modifiers. Mixed ESG track records cannot be hidden in AI answer environments regardless of content quality.
3 major brands with consistent AI caveats
INSIGHT 06
The Mission-Driven Premium
Brands with constitutionally embedded missions average rank 3.3 among citing engines vs. 8.9 for general ESG reporters. AI engines reward purpose clarity. Vague sustainability programs generate lower, less consistent rankings than bold, specific, single-issue commitments.
Avg rank 3.3 mission-led vs. 8.9 general ESG

Engine Intelligence — How Each AI Reasons About Sustainability
Behavioral profiles & sustainability ROI signals by engine
ChatGPT
Narrative
Favors iconic consumer brands with strong public sustainability narratives. Rewards visible environmental missions, brand activism, and mission-led growth stories. Rankings closely mirror mainstream media coverage.
Unique Inclusions
Toyota, Salesforce, Kering, H&M, Starbucks
Consensus Alignment: 72%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“A credible sustainability commitment can increase purchase intent, loyalty, and price tolerance by strengthening trust and brand differentiation. It also improves retailer appeal and repeat sales. However, impact is strongest when claims are visible, verified, and paired with product quality, convenience, and competitive pricing.”
Claude
ESG Analyst
The only engine bridging B2B and consumer worlds. Leads with industrial ESG champions (Schneider Electric, Vestas) while including narrative leaders (Patagonia, Unilever). Prioritizes measurable, verifiable environmental impact.
Unique Inclusions
Sims Limited, Johnson Controls, Burt's Bees, HP Inc., Vestas
Consensus Alignment: 58%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“A strong sustainability commitment significantly boosts brand appeal and consumer trust, leading to increased sales. Ethically-minded consumers, particularly younger demographics, actively seek out and reward brands that align with their environmental and social values, making sustainable practices a key differentiator.”
Gemini
Mission Curator
Uniquely values mission-driven niche brands alongside institutional leaders. The only engine to prominently feature Seventh Generation and Allbirds. Weights commitment intensity and brand purpose alongside scale.
Unique Inclusions
Seventh Generation, Eileen Fisher, Ben & Jerry's, Novo Nordisk, Allbirds
Consensus Alignment: 68%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“A strong sustainability commitment drives sales by building consumer trust and brand loyalty. Modern shoppers, particularly younger demographics, actively seek out eco-friendly products. They are often willing to pay a premium for ethical brands, directly boosting revenue while differentiating the company in a competitive market.”
Grok
Metrics-First
Rankings dominated by industrial/B2B companies with measurable ESG performance data. Most divergent from consumer-brand consensus. Anchors to formal ESG index methodology.
Unique Inclusions
Constellation Energy, Ormat Technologies, Keysight, AptarGroup, Beko
Consensus Alignment: 22%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“A brand's commitment to sustainability tangibly impacts sales and purchasing decisions by enhancing brand reputation, attracting ethically conscious consumers, and fostering loyalty through perceived shared values. This can lead to increased market share and premium pricing.”
Llama
Brand Recognizer
Mirrors ChatGPT consumer-brand orientation but with broader global coverage. Includes Coca-Cola, Enel, and Stella McCartney. Demonstrates international brand recognition beyond US/European focus.
Unique Inclusions
The Body Shop, Reebok, Enel, Stella McCartney, Coca-Cola
Consensus Alignment: 65%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“A brand's commitment to sustainability tangibly boosts sales as consumers increasingly prefer eco-friendly products, driving purchasing decisions towards brands that demonstrate strong environmental and social responsibility, thereby enhancing brand loyalty and reputation.”
Copilot
Consensus Builder
Produces rankings closest to cross-engine consensus. Overlaps substantially with ChatGPT, Gemini, and DeepSeek. Its unique additions (BMW, Roche, Meta) suggest a slightly broader corporate sustainability lens.
Unique Inclusions
BMW, Sims Limited, Roche, Meta, Sanofi
Consensus Alignment: 78%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“Strong sustainability commitments build trust, justify price premiums, reduce churn, and attract conscious consumers—especially younger buyers—directly lifting revenue and brand preference.”
Perplexity
Index Purist
The data-index extreme. Cited Corporate Knights Global 100 exclusively, producing near-zero overlap with other engines in positions 1-12. Represents a fundamental epistemological divergence.
Unique Inclusions
ERG SpA, EDP Renováveis, XPeng, Telus, Severn Trent
Consensus Alignment: 18%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“Sustainability commitment boosts sales by 20–30% via consumer preference for ethical brands, influencing 78% of purchases per Nielsen studies, enhancing loyalty and premium pricing power.”
DeepSeek
Global Surveyor
Aligns closely with ChatGPT and Copilot but adds a distinctly global lens. Includes Samsung, Sony, Intel, and Mercedes-Benz. Reflects broader international training data.
Unique Inclusions
Samsung, Intel, Sony, Mercedes-Benz, L'Oréal
Consensus Alignment: 70%
How Sustainability Impacts Sales & Purchasing
“A brand's commitment to sustainability tangibly boosts sales by enhancing reputation, attracting values-driven consumers, fostering loyalty, and differentiating products. Consumers increasingly prioritize ethical and eco-friendly choices, directly influencing their purchasing decisions and willingness to pay a premium.”

Narrative-First (5 Engines)
ChatGPT, Gemini,
Llama, Copilot, DeepSeek
Favor consumer-brand storytelling, public ESG narratives, and media-driven reputation. These engines reward visible sustainability commitments and brand activism.
Data-First (2 Engines)
Grok & Perplexity
Anchor to quantitative ESG indices. Their rankings feature industrial/B2B companies with measurable sustainable revenue ratios, often invisible to narrative engines.

Claude

△ The Answer Divide
Is Your Brand Built for the Answer Economy — or Still Speaking to Search Engines?
In the Answer Economy, the question is no longer whether your brand appears in search results — it’s whether AI answer engines can find, trust, and cite your sustainability story with confidence. The Answer Divide separates brands whose digital infrastructure actively feeds AI answers from those whose content architecture remains stuck in a pre-AI paradigm. Each brand below is assessed on Overall Answer Friendliness and Sustainability Answer Readiness, based on direct website research beyond the screenshots shown.
Answer Absent
0–4.0
Not structured for AI extraction
Emerging
4.1–6.0
Some signals, gaps remain
Answer Ready
6.1–7.9
Structured content, good AI extractability
Answer Optimized
8.0–9.9
Built for AI-first discovery
Answer Leader
10
Benchmark-setting
Schneider Electric
9.8
Overall AF
9.8
Sustain. AR
AI Insights module live190+ sustainability products99+ docs indexed33 dedicated FAQ results
Answer Divide Analysis
The most Answer-Friendly sustainability brand in this study. Schneider has built an AI-native ecosystem: the perspectives.se.com blog publishes structured sustainability articles with named executive quotations monthly — exactly the format AI engines need to cite confidently. The Resource Advisor+ Copilot answers sustainability questions in natural language. CEO Olivier Blum’s public statements appear verbatim across dozens of indexed documents. With 75% Scope 1&2 emissions reduction (SBTi-verified), 190+ sustainability-tagged products, 99+ documents, and an embedded AI Insights search module, Schneider has not just closed the Answer Divide — they are defining what answer-native enterprise content looks like.
AI Insights + Conversational Copilot75% Scope 1&2 Reduction VerifiedAnswer Leader 9.8/10
Overall AF
Sustainability AR
Microsoft
9.2
Overall AF
8.0
Sustain. AR
Copilot Chat embeddedAI-first Support UXCarbon Negative by 2030Sustainability content siloed
Answer Divide Analysis
Microsoft’s Answer Friendliness carries a structural advantage no other brand has: it literally powers several of the engines ranking it. As the developer of Copilot, Microsoft’s sustainability content is natively ingested into ranking infrastructure. Annual Environmental Sustainability Reports with full Scope 1-3 data and an internal carbon fee program (charging business divisions for emissions) are documented with enough specificity to be AI-cited as mechanisms, not aspirations. However, the support page shows the gap: sustainability content requires deliberate navigation to find. If integrated at the same prominence as product categories, Microsoft’s Answer Share would likely approach 65%+.
Powers Copilot Answer InfrastructureCarbon Negative 2030 PledgeSustainability Microsite Gap

Unilever
9.0
Overall AF
9.4
Sustain. AR
703 sustainability resultsContent-type filteringDedicated Sustainability navInternal AI sustainability bot
Answer Divide Analysis
Unilever’s 703 sustainability results are not just volume — they represent a content architecture engineered for AI extraction depth. Our research confirms Unilever has deployed an internal AI chatbot (built on Microsoft Copilot) specifically to mine its own sustainability data, demonstrating organizational AI literacy at the deepest operational level. Their sustainable living brands (Dove, Hellmann’s, Seventh Generation) have grown 69% faster than the rest of the business — a specific, verifiable, commercially-linked statistic appearing verbatim in multiple engine rationales. This is the benchmark: sustainability is not a section of the Unilever brand — it is the lens through which everything is published.
703 Sustainability ResultsInternal AI Sustainability ChatbotSustainable Brands 69% Faster Growth
Overall AF
Sustainability AR
Nestlé
7.2
Overall AF
5.8
Sustain. AR
"Ask Nestlé" conversational formatSustainability top-nav itemCSV Annual ReportPersistent credibility qualifier
Answer Divide Analysis
"Ask Nestlé" is the right architecture for the Answer Economy — but the credibility gap AI engines surface is not primarily a content problem, and that distinction matters. Nestlé publishes a comprehensive Creating Shared Value report with Scope 1-3 data, regenerative agriculture progress for 1 million farms, and net-zero 2050 targets. The Q&A format mirrors AI output structure and is highly extractable. However, AI systems have absorbed decades of media coverage around water rights, baby formula, and deforestation controversies, and weight this against current claims. Nestlé’s most powerful Answer Economy move is a "Where We’ve Fallen Short and What We’ve Changed" content track — radical transparency about past failures. AI engines reward verifiable accountability as much as achievement.
"Ask Nestlé" Answer ArchitectureNet-Zero 2050 DocumentedCredibility Gap: Persistent AI Qualifier

IKEA
7.5
Overall AF
7.0
Sustain. AR
Conversational Q&A formatClean navigation structureClimate-positive by 2030FAQ not used for sustainability
Answer Divide Analysis
IKEA has significantly more answer-friendly infrastructure than the snapshot reveals. Their People & Planet Positive strategy documents named 2030 climate-positive targets. IKEA sources 98% of wood from more sustainable sources and scores 91/100 in InfluenceMap’s climate policy engagement rankings — ahead of most consumer brands. The FAQ screenshot reveals a genuine architectural opportunity: IKEA’s conversational “Have a question? We are happy to help” format uses exactly the language pattern AI engines are built around — but it’s deployed for operational queries, not sustainability leadership. Creating a "Sustainability Q&A" section with the same format and specific figures would dramatically improve AI extractability and likely boost Answer Share from 56.9% toward 65%+.
Climate Policy Engagement: 91/100Answer Ready 7.5/10FAQ Architecture Under-Used
Overall AF
Sustainability AR
Apple
7.8
Overall AF
6.2
Sustain. AR
Premium structured UXProduct-category navigationNo sustainability surfacedLifecycle carbon data in microsite
Answer Divide Analysis
Apple’s sustainability content depth is world-class — the problem is architectural isolation. Apple publishes product-level Environmental Progress Reports for every device, calculating lifecycle carbon footprints to the gram. The Supplier Clean Energy Program covers 320+ suppliers across 30 countries. Annual Environmental Progress Reports run 50+ pages with third-party verified data. But the support page shows the failure: a user’s primary Apple experience sees zero sustainability content. Contrast with Patagonia, where sustainability is the product page. Apple’s rich environment content gets lower AI extraction weight than its depth deserves. The fix is executable: integrate carbon footprint badges and recycled material percentages directly onto product pages. Apple has the data; it needs to surface it where AI engines look first.
Product-Level Carbon Reports Per Device320+ Supplier Clean Energy ProgramSustainability Architecturally Isolated
Overall AF
Sustainability AR
Tesla
5.5
Overall AF
4.0
Sustain. AR
Product support focus onlyIRA compliance doc onlyNo sustainability narrativeMission-content mismatch
Answer Divide Analysis
Tesla represents the most severe brand-to-content mismatch in this study. The mission — accelerating the world’s transition to sustainable energy — is the most powerful sustainability statement in the automotive category. Yet the support page’s trending topics (Supercharging Credits, Leasing, Troubleshooting) are entirely operational. The IRA Clean Vehicle Report is a government compliance document, not a sustainability narrative. Our research shows Tesla’s annual Impact Report is significantly harder to discover and less richly structured than competitors. More critically, Tesla’s brand has become entangled with Elon Musk’s controversies in AI training data, creating conflicting signals. This explains why Tesla appears in only 3 of 8 engines. The fix: a prominent sustainability hub at tesla.com/impact, structured Scope 1-3 data, and a dedicated Q&A section. The mission is there. The content architecture needs to match it.
Emerging 5.5/10 — Largest GapMission-Content MismatchConflicting Brand Signals in AI Data
Overall AF
Sustainability AR
Patagonia
9.6
Overall AF
9.9
Sustain. AR
Google AI Mode now liveMission = Product = Content130-page 2025 Progress ReportWorn Wear + Footprint Chronicles
Answer Divide Analysis
Patagonia doesn’t optimize for sustainability answers — it IS the sustainability answer. The Google AI Mode screenshot (April 24, 2026) reveals exactly why this matters: with AI Mode embedded in the world’s most-visited search page, Patagonia’s decades of structured sustainability content now feed into an AI answer layer reaching billions of queries daily. Their 2025 Work in Progress Report is 130 pages of SBTi-verified emissions tracking, Fair Trade certification data, and supply chain disclosure. The Footprint Chronicles traces every product to source. Spring 2025 completed a decade-long PFAS phase-out. 86% of materials are preferred. 95%+ of products are Fair Trade certified. Each claim is specific, dated, verifiable, and third-party certified: the four pillars of maximum AI citability. Patagonia earns 9.9 Sustainability Answer Readiness not because it is perfect — emissions rose 2% in FY2025 — but because it makes complexity transparently findable.
#1 in 5 Engines — 71.3% Answer Share130-Page 2025 Progress ReportAnswer Leader 9.6/10
Overall AF
Sustainability AR
The Answer Divide: Key Takeaways
Who’s Winning
Schneider Electric and Unilever have built AI-native content ecosystems. Patagonia’s entire brand is the sustainability content. These brands have closed the Answer Divide at the content layer.
Who’s at Risk
Apple and Tesla have rich sustainability programs locked behind architecture that doesn’t surface them. Their Answer Divide is a distribution problem — not a substance problem.
The Imperative
Every brand needs a dedicated Answer Friendliness audit: structured sustainability Q&A pages, schema-marked ESG data, third-party citation pathways, and content designed for AI extraction, not just human reading.

Company Profiles
30 brands · 100-word profiles with scorecard data
Filter:
Patagonia
Outdoor / Consumer
6 engines71.3%
Founded in 1973 by Yvon Chouinard, Patagonia is the uncontested benchmark for brand-level sustainability commitment. The company donates all profits to environmental causes and pioneered the “Don’t Buy This Jacket” anti-consumerism campaign. Worn Wear enables repair over replacement. Patagonia earned the highest Answer Share in this study — 71.3% — ranking #1 across ChatGPT, Gemini, Llama, Copilot, and DeepSeek simultaneously. Its 2025 Work in Progress Report (130 pages), SBTi-verified targets, and fully transparent product-level sustainability data make it the benchmark for AI answer-ready sustainability content. AI engines reward its singular, constitutionally embedded purpose above all other brands.
#1 in 5 EnginesMission-LedAnswer Leader
Schneider Electric
Energy Management / B2B
7 engines66.9%
A global leader in energy management and industrial automation, Schneider Electric has embedded sustainability into every product and service layer. With 190+ sustainability-tagged products, 99+ documents, and an AI Insights module built directly into site search, it is the most answer-ready B2B brand in this study. The only company ranked by 7 of 8 engines and #1 in two (Claude and Grok), Schneider bridges both narrative and data-driven AI epistemologies. They have reduced Scope 1 and 2 emissions 75% since 2017 (SBTi-verified) and consistently rank in the Corporate Knights Global 100.
7/8 Engines#1 in Claude & GrokAI Insights Embedded
Unilever
Consumer Goods
6 engines66.3%
Unilever’s Compass strategy sets measurable targets across climate, nature, plastic, livelihoods, and health. A search for “sustainability” on their website returns 703 results. They have deployed an internal AI chatbot (built on Microsoft Copilot) to mine their own sustainability data. Their sustainable living brands (Dove, Hellmann’s, Seventh Generation) have grown 69% faster than the rest of the portfolio — a commercially-linked stat that AI engines cite repeatedly. Seventh Generation, the purpose-driven household and personal care brand, is a particularly strong AI signal contributor: its carbon-neutral certified products, plant-based formulations, and decades of environmental advocacy generate highly structured, citable sustainability content that reinforces Unilever’s overall Answer Share. Unilever eliminated 100,000+ tonnes of plastic packaging since 2019 and achieved carbon-neutral production on five continents.
703 Sustainability ResultsTop-6 All EnginesContent Depth Leader
IKEA
Home Retail / Consumer
6 engines56.9%
IKEA aims to become fully circular and climate-positive by 2030. The company sources 98% of its wood from more sustainable sources and powers operations with renewable energy. Its global refurbishment and buy-back program is one of the world’s most advanced circular retail implementations. Ranked by 6 engines consistently in the top 6, IKEA’s scale advantage — 775 million store visits annually — gives its sustainability commitments outsized AI citation weight. Climate policy advocates score IKEA 91/100 in corporate climate engagement rankings.
6 Engine ConsensusCircularity Focus2030 Climate-Positive
Microsoft
Technology / Platform
6 engines50.6%
Microsoft has pledged to become carbon negative by 2030 and remove all historical emissions by 2050. It charges an internal carbon fee across all divisions and publishes comprehensive Environmental Sustainability Reports annually. Critically, Microsoft is both a sustainability-credentialed brand and the infrastructure underlying answer engines via Copilot — a unique dual role that likely amplifies its AI citation frequency. Ranked by 6 engines with an average position of 7.5.
Carbon Negative 2030Copilot-Integrated UX6 Engine Consensus
Orsted
Renewable Energy
5 engines48.1%
Ørsted is the world’s most transformative corporate sustainability story: a former coal company that became the global offshore wind leader. It divested all fossil fuel assets and built the world’s largest offshore wind portfolio. The Corporate Knights Global 100 consistently ranks Ørsted among the most sustainable companies. Ranked by 5 engines — and #4 in Perplexity’s data-driven index — it is the strongest renewable energy narrative in AI sustainability verdicts.
Coal-to-Wind Pioneer#4 Perplexity ESG IndexNet-Zero Pathway
Apple
Consumer Technology
5 engines37.5%
Apple publishes product-level Environmental Progress Reports for every device, calculating lifecycle carbon footprints to the gram. The Supplier Clean Energy Program covers 320+ suppliers. Annual Environmental Progress Reports run 50+ pages with third-party verified data. Despite this depth, Apple’s primary digital experience (support pages) surfaces no sustainability content, creating an Answer Divide gap. AI engines rank Apple mid-tier (avg rank ~9 across 5 engines) — underperforming given the quality and depth of underlying content. This is a distribution problem, not a substance problem.
Product-Level Carbon ReportsSustainability Siloed320+ Supplier Clean Energy
Natura & Co
Beauty / Consumer
4 engines36.9%
Natura & Co is the world’s largest B Corp-certified beauty group, encompassing Natura, Avon, The Body Shop, and Aesop. Its Amazon Program partners with 38 communities across Brazil, sourcing 36 bioingredients while supporting forest conservation. The company was the first major cosmetics company to achieve carbon-neutral certification. Ranked by 4 engines with an average rank of 6.3, it is AI sustainability’s beauty sector champion, rewarded for the specificity and verifiability of its Amazon biodiversity commitment.
B Corp CertifiedAmazon BiodiversityCarbon Neutral
Google
Technology / Platform
4 engines33.1%
Google achieved carbon neutrality in 2007 and has matched 100% of electricity use with renewable purchases since 2017. Its goal is 24/7 carbon-free energy across all data centers by 2030 — a significantly harder target than annual matching. Critically, Google is now integrating AI Mode into its primary search bar, making Google simultaneously a sustainability-ranked brand and the platform reshaping how sustainability authority is discovered.
Carbon Neutral Since 200724/7 Carbon-Free Energy GoalAI Mode Integration
Danone
Food & Beverage
5 engines31.9%
Danone became the first listed company in France to adopt “entreprise à mission” (purpose company) status, embedding sustainability into its legal charter. It achieved B Corp certification across its North American zone and committed to a 1.5°C-aligned science-based net-zero target. Danone’s regenerative agriculture program covers 1.3 million hectares. Ranked by 5 engines, consistently cited for its B Corp status and water stewardship.
B Corp CertifiedRegenerative AgricultureWater Stewardship
Interface
Commercial Flooring / B2B
4 engines27.5%
Interface pioneered corporate sustainability with Mission Zero (1994), achieving zero environmental footprint by 2020. The successor Climate Take Back mission creates flooring products that are carbon negative across their full lifecycle. One of only a handful of manufacturers anywhere to achieve net-negative embodied carbon. Ranked by 4 engines including ChatGPT at #2, Interface is AI’s most recognized B2B manufacturing sustainability leader.
Carbon-Negative ProductsClimate Take Back MissionMission Zero Pioneer
Nestle
Food & Beverage
4 engines25.6%
Nestlé has committed to net-zero GHG emissions by 2050 with a 2030 interim target to halve absolute emissions. Its regenerative agriculture program covers 1 million farms. Despite this ambition, Nestlé consistently receives AI credibility caveats reflecting historical controversies. Its “Ask Nestlé” FAQ page is structurally answer-friendly but needs stronger sustainability-specific content and radical transparency to close the credibility gap AI engines keep surfacing.
"Ask Nestlé" PlatformCredibility Asterisk1M Farm Regenerative Program
Tesla
Electric Vehicles
3 engines24.4%
Tesla’s mission — accelerating the world’s transition to sustainable energy — is arguably the most powerful sustainability brand statement in automotive history. Yet it ranks in only 3 of 8 AI engines. Governance concerns, Elon Musk’s polarizing public presence, and a support website optimized for product service rather than sustainability storytelling all contribute. Tesla’s Answer Divide score is the lowest among major consumer brands studied.
3 Engines OnlyBrand-Content MismatchEV Category Pioneer
Brambles
Supply Chain / B2B
3 engines23.1%
Brambles operates CHEP, the world’s leading pallet and container pooling business — a fundamentally circular model that eliminates single-use packaging across global supply chains. Its shared-use platform serves 300,000+ customers across 60 countries, preventing 3.2 million tonnes of CO² annually. Ranked by 3 engines including Claude (#4) and Grok (#4), it is one of only two B2B supply-chain brands achieving meaningful AI sustainability consensus.
Circular Economy Model#4 in Claude & GrokZero Waste Pioneer
Vestas
Wind Energy / B2B
3 engines21.9%
Vestas is the world’s largest wind turbine manufacturer with over 185 GW installed across 90 countries. The company has committed to zero-waste turbines by 2040 and is pioneering blade recyclability. Vestas achieves carbon neutrality across its operations annually. Ranked by 3 engines with Claude placing it #2, Vestas is AI’s most recognized pure-play renewable energy equipment maker.
Wind Energy Pioneer#2 in ClaudeZero-Waste Turbines by 2040
Siemens
Industrial Technology / B2B
5 engines13.1%
Siemens presents this study’s most revealing paradox: cited by 5 of 8 engines (more than Apple or Google) but averaging rank 16.4 — the lowest authority-to-visibility ratio. Siemens achieved carbon neutrality in its own operations ten years ahead of schedule. Yet AI engines consistently fail to elevate it above stronger narratives. Awareness without authority is a critical brand vulnerability in AI answer environments.
5 Engines — Low AuthorityAvg Rank 16.4Floor Visibility Trap
Nike
Sportswear / Consumer
4 engines10.6%
Nike’s Move to Zero program targets zero carbon and zero waste globally. It has incorporated 38% recycled polyester into its products and operates a circular shoe take-back program. Yet every citing engine adds a credibility modifier around labor practices and overconsumption. AI systems synthesize reputational context alongside environmental claims — strong sustainability programs don’t neutralize persistent supply-chain controversies in AI verdicts.
Move to Zero InitiativeCredibility AsteriskMaterials Innovation
LEGO
Toys / Consumer
2 engines11.3%
LEGO has invested over $400 million in a Sustainable Materials Center focused on plant-based and recycled-plastic alternatives. The company powers all global operations with renewable energy. LEGO appears in ChatGPT (#10) and Claude (#18) rankings, cited for long-term generational trust alongside materials innovation. Absent from data-driven engines, LEGO is a narrative AI sustainability winner driven by brand affinity and visible R&D investment.
Sustainable Materials MissionRenewable Energy InvestorLong-Term Trust Brand
Adidas
Sportswear / Consumer
2 engines9.4%
Adidas has produced over 30 million pairs of shoes using Parley Ocean Plastic — yarn made from intercepted marine waste. It targets 9 out of 10 articles using sustainable materials by 2025. Ranked by ChatGPT (#11) and Llama (#12), it earns AI sustainability recognition for tangible product-level materials innovation. The Parley narrative — a single bold partnership — generates durable AI citation value through its specificity and verifiability.
Parley for the Oceans30M+ Ocean Plastic Shoes2025 Circular Target
Kering
Luxury Fashion
2 engines8.8%
Kering — home to Gucci, Saint Laurent, Bottega Veneta — pioneered Environmental Profit & Loss (EP&L) accounting, assigning financial values to environmental impacts across its supply chain. This tool has been adopted by regulators and other companies worldwide. Kering has committed to a 40% reduction in absolute environmental impact. Ranked by ChatGPT (#12) and Gemini (#16), it is AI’s leading luxury sustainability brand — recognized for structural innovation in environmental accounting.
EP&L Accounting PioneerLuxury Sector LeaderRegenerative Sourcing
ABB
Electrification / B2B
2 engines13.1%
ABB is a global leader in electrification and automation, committed to carbon neutrality in its operations by 2030. Its products directly enable customer decarbonization — from EV charging to industrial motor efficiency. Ranked by Claude (#15) and Grok (#6), ABB is one of only a few electrification infrastructure brands achieving cross-engine AI sustainability citation, reflecting measurable systemic impact over brand storytelling.
Electrification Leader2030 Carbon Neutral#6 in Grok
Starbucks
Food & Beverage / Retail
2 engines7.5%
Starbucks sources 99% of coffee through its C.A.F.E. Practices program and has committed to building 10,000 “Greener Stores” globally. Its Planet Positive strategy targets a 50% reduction in carbon, water, and waste by 2030. However, both citing engines (ChatGPT #19, DeepSeek #11) flag single-use cup waste as a persistent liability. Starbucks illustrates how a strong sourcing narrative can be undermined by a single high-visibility operational issue.
Ethical Sourcing C.A.F.E.Packaging Footprint Risk10,000 Greener Stores
Amazon
E-Commerce / Technology
2 engines6.3%
Amazon founded The Climate Pledge in 2019 and operates the world’s largest corporate renewable energy portfolio. Yet both citing engines (ChatGPT #20, Copilot #20) rank it last with identical qualifiers: labor conditions and overconsumption concerns. Amazon proves that scale of investment does not override AI perception of systemic contradiction — being the world’s largest delivery operation while pledging climate leadership creates a credibility tension AI engines consistently surface.
Credibility AsteriskClimate Pledge FounderLabor Concern Qualifier
Toyota
Automotive
1 engines6.9%
Toyota pioneered mass-market hybrid technology with the Prius (20+ million units sold since 1997). Its hydrogen fuel cell vehicle, the Mirai, represents a long-term bet on alternative clean propulsion. However, Toyota has faced criticism for lobbying against stringent EV mandates. ChatGPT ranks it #13, noting "transition pace debated" — the only engine to cite it, reflecting genuine divergence about whether Toyota’s hybrid legacy constitutes sustainability leadership.
Hybrid Scale PioneerBEV Transition DebateChatGPT Only
BMW
Automotive
2 engines7.5%
BMW Group is expanding its BEV lineup aggressively, targeting 50% of global sales as fully electric by 2030. Its circular economy program recycles 95% of end-of-life vehicle materials. BMW’s supply chain standards for conflict minerals and responsible cobalt sourcing are among the most rigorous in the industry. Ranked by Copilot (#14) and DeepSeek (#13), BMW earns AI sustainability recognition for the specificity of its circularity and supply-chain commitments.
Circularity Pioneer50% EV Sales by 2030Supply Chain Standards
About BrandRank.AI: BrandRank works with the world’s top global brands to strengthen trust and purchase intent by uncovering vulnerabilities, purchase-driving insights, and growth opportunities across AI answer engines. As a SaaS platform, we track all major answer engines to improve visibility, ensure content is AI-ready, and rigorously measure brand claims and instructional accuracy.
